mcq.question: What is an input device?
mcq.correct: A device used to enter data into a computer system
wrong: A device used to present data to the user
A device used to save data for later use
A device used to transfer data between networks

mcq.question: Why are input devices needed?
mcq.correct: To allow data and instructions to be entered into a system
wrong: To display information to the user
To store large amounts of data
To improve processor performance

mcq.question: Which of these is a manual input device?
wrong: Monitor
Projector
Printer

mcq.question: How does an optical mouse detect movement?
mcq.correct: Using a light source and optical sensor
wrong: Using a rolling ball and sensors
Using sound waves to judge distance
Using a magnetic disk to track position

mcq.question: Why is an ADC needed with a microphone?
mcq.correct: To convert analogue sound into digital data
wrong: To increase the volume of the sound
To save the sound permanently in memory
To send the sound directly to an output device

mcq.question: What is one advantage of barcodes?
mcq.correct: They can be scanned faster than entering data manually
wrong: They can store moving images
They always take up more space than text
They can only be read by specialised cameras

mcq.question: What is one advantage of QR codes compared with barcodes?
wrong: They can only store numeric data
They must be scanned with one specific reader
They can only be scanned from one angle

mcq.question: What is a 2D scanner used for?
mcq.correct: To convert documents or images into a digital format
wrong: To produce a physical copy of an object
To record changes in heat
To sense force applied to a surface

mcq.question: What does a 3D scanner create?
mcq.correct: A digital representation of a 3D object
wrong: A flat printed copy of the object
A signal used to store data magnetically
An audio file of the object

mcq.question: How does a digital camera capture an image?
mcq.correct: By focusing light onto a CCD that records pixels
wrong: By converting sound waves into electrical signals
By reading microscopic marks on an optical disc
By magnetically reading data from a storage disk
